prediction.attackType: Brute Force (T1110) leading to Valid Accounts (T1078)
prediction.summary: Multiple SSH brute-force attempts were detected targeting privileged accounts 'admin' and 'root' from various source IPs. A critical event occurred when the 'admin' account was successfully compromised from IP 192.168.1.50 after a series of failed password attempts. This indicates a successful brute-force attack and unauthorized access to a privileged account.
recommendations: Immediately block the source IP 192.168.1.50 at the network perimeter (firewall/security group) to prevent further access.
Disable the compromised 'admin' account and force a password reset. Revoke all active sessions for this user.

Initiate a full forensic investigation into the 'admin' session opened from 192.168.1.50 to determine the extent of compromise, including commands executed, files accessed, and any lateral movement attempts.
Review authentication logs for other privileged accounts (e.g., 'root') for similar brute-force attempts or successful logins from suspicious IPs.
Implement stronger authentication mechanisms for SSH, such as key-based authentication and multi-factor authentication (MFA), especially for privileged accounts.
Deploy or configure rate-limiting tools (e.g., fail2ban) to automatically block IPs after a defined number of failed login attempts.
